agent_notes: Barking and Dagenham has real cultural texture but a thin PAINTS-ready venue base; quality output is below the 30-35 target because the borough lacks enough qualifying independent drinks, books-and-coffee, and same-category backup options.
No Creative Walks were included because no Barking and Dagenham park or route appears on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list.
Most included venues are Tier B because the PAINTS backup-radius rule could not be met with a same-category qualifying venue; host should verify live Google ratings/review counts before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Open Thursday, Friday and Saturday 12:00-17:00 per official visit page. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm bank holiday/private event closure.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free public museum; walk-in/drop-in implied by public visiting hours, but group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for a quiet 8-12 person gallery visit during off-peak afternoon; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Step-free public space with seating noted by official visit page; capacity for a facilitated PAINTS group is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday or Friday 13:00-15:00 to avoid Saturday family/event pressure.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small, mission-led museum in Barking Wharf Square gives the borough a feminist cultural anchor rather than a generic gallery stop. The 2026 Materiality of Memory programme and Ruth Ewan exhibition provide enough live content for a reflective group discussion. It is a strong PAINTS fit aesthetically and socially, but the lack of a same-category backup within 10 minutes keeps it Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum grew from the East End Women's Museum project and Barking and Dagenham's culture service, creating a permanent platform for women and girls' histories in a borough usually underrepresented in London culture maps.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Materiality of Memory 2026 programme; Ruth Ewan's Always Something There to Remind Me runs 13 March-12 December 2026, with collected materials and artworks linked to feminist histories and the Dagenham Agates sculptures.
venues.safety_notes: Barking Wharf Square is close to Barking station and the museum states the station is about a 10-minute walk with step-free routes. Use daytime slots and avoid dispersing the group late evening.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - closest plausible cultural alternatives are Eastbury Manor House or Barking Learning Centre Gallery, both require host verification and/or exceed the PAINTS backup standard.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current programme is strong but group capacity, walk-in handling for 8-12, live Google rating/review count, and private-event closures must be confirmed.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Eastbury Square, Upney station / Barking station
venues.opening_hours: Official Instagram says Thursday-Friday 10:00-16:00 and Sundays 11:00-16:00; official visit page asks groups to enquire. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm exact public opening date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public open days appear walk-in/ticketed depending on programme; group visits require enquiry. Treat as reservation-required for PAINTS and keep Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for 8-12 if the house confirms group handling; otherwise too fragile for spontaneous matching.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic house with gardens and event programme; group visit process exists, but same-day group walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 11:30-13:30 or Friday 10:30-12:30, depending on confirmed opening.
venues.why_fits_paints: Eastbury is the borough's most atmospheric heritage interior: a Grade I Elizabethan gentry house in an unexpected residential pocket of Barking. Tracing Together links the house's historical tapestry logic to contemporary digital community memory. It is culturally excellent but operationally weak for PAINTS because opening days are limited and the backup requirement is not met.
venues.anecdote_story: Built between 1560 and 1573, Eastbury is a rare surviving mid-16th-century brick gentry house; its local mythology includes long-running Gunpowder Plot rumours, which the house itself discusses in its journals.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Tracing Together by Compiler relocated from the Women's Museum to Eastbury Manor House, turning local journeys and overlooked routes into a shared digital tapestry; listed 13 May-4 July 2026 by Women's Museum and spring/summer 2026 by Eastbury.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime only. The house is in a residential setting; solo arrivals should be routed from Upney or Barking by well-lit main roads.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - Women's Museum is a stronger backup culturally but is not within the PAINTS 10-minute walking radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Tracing Together ends on 4 July 2026 per Women's Museum page, so host must confirm whether it remains visitable after run date; opening hours and group walk-in handling are limited.

venues.opening_hours: 2026 official hours: Tuesday-Thursday and Saturday 10:00-16:00; Monday and Friday closed. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date closures.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum with public opening hours and drop-in events; group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for a small daytime group if the museum confirms; tea room/archives could support pacing.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum, archive, gardens and Tulip Tree Tearoom; public group handling is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.why_fits_paints: Valence House gives PAINTS a lived-history angle: manor house, moat, borough archive, Becontree estate memory and local artefacts in one place. The permanent displays are strong enough for a reflective museum day, even though the most recent Brilliant Trees exhibition had closed by the run date. The lack of a current major exhibition and no same-category nearby backup keep it Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Valence is the only surviving one of Dagenham's five manor houses and later served as a civic and library building before becoming the borough museum and archive.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Permanent borough-history galleries, Dagenham Idol, local studies/archive material, gardens and 2026 family/heritage event programme; Brilliant Trees closed 6 June 2026 but related photography walk is listed for 1 August.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime slots recommended. Valence Park is not on the PAINTS Creative Walk safety list, so use the museum as the destination rather than a park-based walk.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - The White House is culturally related but lacks a verified current public exhibition/opening schedule.
venues.risks_watchouts: The strongest current temporary exhibition evidence is dated before run date; host should confirm what is physically on view before programming.

venues.opening_hours: Official council page: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday and Saturday 09:00-17:00; closed on Bank Holidays.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access public street market; walk-in is inherent. Meeting point must be separately confirmed if used.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a loose browsing event for 8-12, not a seated/talk-heavy format.
venues.capacity_notes: Street market with around 60 stalls per NMTF and over 100 trading positions per heritage page; group should meet outside the busiest stall flow.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:30-12:30 for fuller atmosphere; weekday 10:30-12:00 for quieter conversation.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is not a pretty farmers' market, but it has real borough grain: old charter history, civic management, independent traders and a lived high-street pulse. It works best as a social-observation market day paired with Barking Abbey/Women's Museum context rather than a pure food pilgrimage. It is Tier B because the market is more practical than curated and no same-category backup exists nearby.
venues.anecdote_story: Barking's market right dates to the 12th century; the current town-centre market was revived in 1991 and sits on East Street and surrounding streets.
venues.serve_or_on_view: General street-market stalls, food and independent traders; not producer-led enough for Tier A.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime town-centre setting close to Barking station. Avoid closing time dispersal and keep a visible meeting point.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying market/craft backup found within 15 min walk or transit in-borough; Dagenham Sunday Market is closed.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying market backup within 15 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Curatorial atmosphere is uneven and weather exposed; host should confirm stall mix on intended date.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Abbey Road / Barking Riverside edge, Barking station
venues.opening_hours: Instagram lists Tuesday-Friday 16:00-22:00 and Saturday-Sunday 12:00-22:00; CAMRA lists Monday 17:00-23:00, Tuesday-Friday 16:00-22:00; AccessAble has older all-week hours. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Appears to operate as a pub/bar with walk-ins, but group of 8-12 seating/walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely possible early evening or weekend afternoon if tables are available; call ahead strongly advised.
venues.capacity_notes: Skiddle lists capacity 75; AccessAble notes lift/toilet access but advises calling ahead for lift needs.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Boathouse is one of Barking's few independent-feeling social venues with a riverside/creative texture and community-business story. It has enough character for a show-and-tell drinks night if PAINTS wants the borough represented beyond heritage museums. It is not a natural-wine bar and has no qualifying drinks backup within 10 minutes, so Tier B only.
venues.anecdote_story: BD Giving profiled owner Derrick and described the venue as a small Barking and Dagenham business that survived pandemic pressure; The Boathouse Barking CIC also appears at the same Granary address.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Beer, cocktails, food and community-event atmosphere; not a specialist wine list.
venues.safety_notes: Use earlier evening slots. The riverside/industrial edge is interesting but solo departures after dark should be routed back toward Barking station together.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying independent drinks backup found within 10 min - nearby options are either not same-category PAINTS fit, chain-led, or unverified.
venues.risks_watchouts: Management/hours signals vary across sources; PAINTS must call to confirm opening, group walk-in, and current food/drinks offer.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA lists Monday-Thursday 10:00-23:00, Friday-Saturday 11:00-midnight, Sunday 12:00-22:30. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm with pub/Facebook.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub walk-ins likely; group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable for a heritage-pub conversation if a side room/table area is available; call ahead required.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic multi-room interior with music/performance area described by pub-history reviewers; exact usable group capacity is UNVERIFIED.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 17:00-19:00 or Saturday 14:00-16:00, avoiding late-night entertainment peaks.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Admiral Vernon is culturally interesting because the building itself is the show-and-tell object: a Grade II 1930s Brewers' Tudor pub with preserved historic interiors. It is a distinctive counterweight to central London wine bars and can anchor a conversation about inter-war suburban pub culture. It does not meet the natural-wine/independent-gastro ideal and lacks a close backup, so it remains Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Historic England listed the Admiral Vernon at Grade II in 2022, and Stonegate describes it as one of London's finest surviving examples of a 1930s Brewers' Tudor inter-war pub.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Traditional pub drinks and preserved inter-war pub interior; no verified natural wine or craft-led list.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime/early evening only; have attendees travel in pairs to/from the nearest transit stops.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - Cross Keys is the closest PAINTS-coded alternative but exceeds the 10-minute walk standard.
venues.risks_watchouts: The pub is heritage-rich but may feel local/rough-edged rather than PAINTS-soft; host must confirm current atmosphere, Google rating/reviews, and group seating.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA describes it as open daily 11:00-23:00; exact event-date hours are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub walk-ins likely; group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible if host secures a quiet corner; not suitable for a refined wine-bar format.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic pub with events hall noted by Film Barking and Dagenham; current group use/capacity is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Cross Keys has the strongest old-building story among the borough's pubs: timber frame, Dagenham Village setting, and a claim to being one of the oldest buildings in the borough. It can support a show-and-tell prompt about continuity, village remnants and suburban London history. Drinks curation is weak by PAINTS standards and backup is missing, so it barely clears the Tier B floor.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA describes the building as 15th-century, Grade II listed and timber-framed, becoming an inn soon after 1700; Film Barking and Dagenham notes original beams, leaded windows and wood-panelled interiors.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Traditional pub drinks in a historic timber-framed building; no verified natural wine or specialist drinks list.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime/early evening only. The setting is more residential and less transit-rich than Barking town centre, so plan arrivals/departures carefully.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - Admiral Vernon is a strong cultural alternative but outside the 10-minute backup radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Local-pub atmosphere may not suit all PAINTS groups; host must confirm current condition, walk-ins, group seating and Google rating/review threshold.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Rejected because Mayesbrook Park is not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list; do not source parks outside the list.
Rejected because Valence Park is not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list, even though it surrounds Valence House.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Barking Abbey / Barking town heritage walk
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Rejected because the route is not an approved PAINTS Creative Walk route; it can support context for a museum/market day but not as a sourced Creative Walk.

Strong cultural venue but rejected for inclusion because no current public exhibition/opening schedule was verified for the run date; official site says it is regularly open for workshops and events rather than standard gallery visits.
Rejected because the visible Pivotal exhibition evidence is old/unclear and current public exhibition hours could not be verified to PAINTS standard.
Excellent historical PAINTS fit, but rejected because sources conflict and Randomness Guide reports Ezo closed in late 2018; current operating status as a bookish cafe is not verified.
Rejected because it is a civic library/cafe rather than an independent bookshop with coffee or a cafe with substantive literary curation; PAINTS Books & Coffee raised bar not met.
Rejected because the market closed permanently in 2020 and therefore cannot be used as a current PAINTS venue or backup.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: The Wilds at Barking Riverside maker/community markets
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Rejected for main list because the source evidence points to periodic/community events rather than a reliable recurring market day with verifiable stall mix on the run date.
Rejected because it is a Wetherspoon pub/chain format, which PAINTS v6 hard-rejects for Tier A and does not fit the independent character drinks brief.
Rejected because it is listed as a Marston's/Brewers Fayre-style chain pub by CAMRA and does not meet the independent, character-led PAINTS drinks requirement.
Rejected because available review signal is weak for PAINTS: Tripadvisor rating is below the Tier B Google-equivalent floor and there is not enough aesthetic/story strength to compensate.
Rejected despite strong independent-bookshop credentials because it is outside Barking and Dagenham and does not solve the in-district Books & Coffee requirement.
